title: Work — cluster surfacing
summary: Hero thesis + full cluster ribbon (8 chips with counts) + 8 anchored per-cluster editorial sections. Per ADR-0009. Replaces v2's flat 9-item slate.

strategy.decision: Hero + cluster ribbon (8 chips, full form, label + count + anchor) + 8 anchored sections (one per cluster, each using its declared composition per ADR-0009 — schematic for forge, card grid for agent-infra, case-study deep-dive for AI-products + commerce + 630, live-URL list for personal-orbit, compact list for client-work, pointer for writing). Footer transition to /practice + /about.
strategy.why: ADR-0009 picked this pattern over bento (categorically refused R10), tabs (hides breadth), filterable matrix (utilitarian), and pure stacked sections (no at-a-glance). The hybrid combines scanning (ribbon) + depth (sections). Counts come from research/current-state/repos-inventory.md — must match.
strategy.shipped: v2 /work — flat 9-item slate: 5 lead case studies + 4 honorable mentions. Hides 60+ repos and the cluster structure entirely.
strategy.gap: Proposed = 8 cluster sections exposing 69 repos + 17 deploys + 275 published artifacts with PUBLIC/private badges per repo; Shipped = 9-item curated slate.
strategy.question: Is the cluster ribbon scannable on mobile (390px) or does it horizontal-scroll into uselessness? Test in current-state capture pass.

currentState.summary: v2 /work — flat slate, 5 lead case studies + 4 honorable mentions.
currentState.annotation: Major IA change: flat slate → 8 cluster ribbon + 8 anchored sections. The page becomes long-scroll editorial; the scroll IS the proof of scope.
